meta.title: GCP-ADP-1: Core Data Services and Governance

questions.stem: A team wants to store raw files cheaply and durably before loading them into analytics systems. Which Google Cloud service is the primary object store for this use case?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Cloud Storage is Google Cloud's managed object storage service for files, data lakes, and staging areas.

**B** is incorrect because BI Engine accelerates BigQuery queries rather than storing raw objects.

**C** is incorrect because Cloud SQL is a relational database service.

**D** is incorrect because Pub/Sub Lite is for messaging, not primary object storage.

[Cloud Storage product overview](https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/introduction)

questions.stem: A dataset is rarely accessed and can tolerate the longest retrieval delay at the lowest storage price. Which Cloud Storage class is the best fit?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Archive is the lowest-cost Cloud Storage class for data accessed very infrequently and with higher retrieval expectations.

**B** is incorrect because Standard is for hot data.

**C** is incorrect because Nearline is for infrequent but more regular access.

**D** is incorrect because Regional SSD is not a Cloud Storage class.

[Cloud Storage storage classes](https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/storage-classes)

questions.stem: Which Google Cloud service is the primary serverless enterprise data warehouse for SQL analytics?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because BigQuery is Google Cloud's serverless analytics warehouse for SQL queries at scale.

**B** is incorrect because Cloud Run runs containers.

**C** is incorrect because Cloud Build builds software.

**D** is incorrect because GKE runs Kubernetes clusters.

[BigQuery introduction](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/introduction)

questions.stem: A team wants to query files in Cloud Storage without first loading them into native BigQuery storage. Which BigQuery feature should they use?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because BigQuery external tables let analysts query data stored outside native BigQuery storage, including Cloud Storage.

**B** is incorrect because BI Engine accelerates queries but does not define storage access.

**C** is incorrect because CMEK secures data but does not make external data queryable.

**D** is incorrect because scheduled queries automate SQL execution, not storage format.

[BigQuery external tables](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/external-tables)

questions.stem: Which SQL capabilities are native to BigQuery? (Select TWO)

options.text: Working with nested and repeated data using `UNNEST`
Indexes must be created before any query can run

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because BigQuery supports analytic SQL features such as `UNNEST` for repeated fields and window functions.

**C** is incorrect because BigQuery supports joins and far more than single-table queries.

**D** is incorrect because BigQuery does not require traditional indexes before querying.

[GoogleSQL query syntax](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/standard-sql/query-syntax)

questions.stem: A team needs a managed stream and batch processing service built on Apache Beam concepts. Which Google Cloud service is the best fit?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Dataflow is Google Cloud's managed service for Apache Beam pipelines across batch and streaming workloads.

**B** is incorrect because CDN serves cached web content.

**C** is incorrect because Secret Manager stores secrets.

**D** is incorrect because Cloud DNS manages DNS zones.

[Dataflow overview](https://cloud.google.com/dataflow/docs/overview)

questions.stem: Which Google service is commonly used to build and share dashboards directly from BigQuery and other connectors?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Looker Studio is Google Cloud's reporting and dashboarding tool with connectors including BigQuery.

**B** is incorrect because Cloud Scheduler triggers jobs.

**C** is incorrect because Artifact Registry stores packages and images.

**D** is incorrect because Datastream replicates databases.

[Looker Studio overview](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/studio/what-you-need-to-use-looker-studio)

questions.stem: A company wants a unified governance layer for data lakes and warehouses, including zones and data quality tasks. Which service should it evaluate first?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Dataplex is the Google Cloud data governance and management service for organizing, securing, and monitoring distributed data.

**B** is incorrect because Cloud Shell is a shell environment.

**C** is incorrect because Memorystore is managed Redis or Memcached.

**D** is incorrect because Cloud Tasks queues asynchronous work.

[Dataplex overview](https://cloud.google.com/dataplex/docs/overview)

questions.stem: Which Google Cloud service helps discover, inspect, and de-identify sensitive information such as PII?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Sensitive Data Protection, formerly Cloud DLP, is the Google Cloud service for inspection, classification, and de-identification of sensitive data.

**B** is incorrect because Cloud Armor is a WAF and DDoS protection service.

**C** is incorrect because Binary Authorization controls container deployment policy.

**D** is incorrect because Cloud Deploy manages release pipelines.

[Sensitive Data Protection overview](https://docs.cloud.google.com/sensitive-data-protection/docs)

questions.stem: A developer needs a high-throughput API for writing records into BigQuery tables in near real time. Which interface is designed for this?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because The BigQuery Storage Write API is built for efficient streaming writes into BigQuery tables.

**B** is incorrect because the Cloud Storage XML API writes objects, not BigQuery rows.

**C** is incorrect because Cloud Logging ingests logs.

**D** is incorrect because Dataproc Serverless is for Spark workloads.

[BigQuery Storage Write API](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/write-api)

questions.stem: A user wants to avoid scanning an entire BigQuery table when filtering by event date. Which design choice helps most directly?

options.text: Store the table in Cloud Storage Archive

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because partitioning lets BigQuery prune data based on the partition filter, reducing scanned bytes.

**B** is incorrect because Archive storage is unrelated to BigQuery table pruning.

**C** is incorrect because Pub/Sub is messaging.

**D** is incorrect because Cloud Run jobs do not change table layout.

[Partitioned tables in BigQuery](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/partitioned-tables)

questions.stem: A table is frequently filtered by `customer_id` after date pruning. Which BigQuery optimization can further improve performance?

options.text: Moving the table to Cloud SQL

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because clustering organizes storage for selective filtering on clustered columns after partition pruning.

**B** is incorrect because Cloud SQL is a different database service.

**C** is incorrect because CDN does not optimize warehouse scans.

**D** is incorrect because IAM Conditions affect access, not physical layout.

[Clustered tables in BigQuery](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/clustered-tables)

questions.stem: Which statements about Cloud Storage classes are correct? (Select TWO)

options.text: Standard is for frequently accessed data
Nearline is intended for infrequently accessed data
Archive is designed for hot analytics data
Storage class changes require rewriting all application SQL

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Standard and Nearline are different access-pattern classes for object storage with different cost characteristics.

**C** is incorrect because Archive is for very cold data.

**D** is incorrect because storage class selection does not rewrite SQL queries.

[Cloud Storage storage classes](https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/storage-classes)

questions.stem: A data engineer wants a fully managed service to replicate database changes continuously into Google Cloud. Which service should they consider first?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Datastream is Google's serverless change data capture and replication service.

**B** is incorrect because Cloud Build builds software.

**C** is incorrect because Filestore is managed NFS.

**D** is incorrect because Interconnect provides network connectivity.

[Datastream overview](https://cloud.google.com/datastream/docs/overview)

questions.stem: Which tasks are a strong fit for Cloud Scheduler? (Select TWO)

options.text: Starting a recurring job on a cron-like schedule
Providing a dashboarding layer for analysts

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Cloud Scheduler runs cron-like scheduled jobs, often by invoking HTTP targets or Pub/Sub.

**C** is incorrect because it does not provide warehouse storage management.

**D** is incorrect because it is not a BI tool.

[Cloud Scheduler overview](https://cloud.google.com/scheduler/docs/overview)

questions.stem: Which access model controls whether a principal can read data in a BigQuery dataset?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because BigQuery access is governed through IAM and BigQuery-specific permissions and roles.

**B** is incorrect because build triggers automate CI/CD.

**C** is incorrect because themes only affect presentation.

**D** is incorrect because Bigtable app profiles are unrelated.

[Access control with IAM in BigQuery](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/access-control)

questions.stem: An analyst wants to email a recurring dashboard snapshot to stakeholders from a reporting tool. Which product best matches that workflow?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Looker Studio is the reporting product used for dashboards and scheduled sharing workflows.

**B** is incorrect because Secret Manager stores secrets.

**C** is incorrect because Dataproc runs Spark/Hadoop.

**D** is incorrect because Cloud Trace is for distributed tracing.

[Looker Studio overview](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/studio/what-you-need-to-use-looker-studio)

questions.stem: A team wants to load CSV files from Cloud Storage into a BigQuery native table on a recurring basis. Which approach is directly supported?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because BigQuery supports load jobs from Cloud Storage into native tables for batch ingestion.

**B** is incorrect because spreadsheet import is not the only method and is not the standard at scale.

**C** is incorrect because Cloud Armor is security middleware.

**D** is incorrect because DaemonSets are a Kubernetes feature.

[Load data into BigQuery](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/loading-data-cloud-storage-csv)

questions.stem: Which features help analyze data in BigQuery? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because BigQuery supports SQL objects and constructs such as views and window functions for analytics.

**C** is incorrect because flow logs are network telemetry.

**D** is incorrect because Cloud NAT rules are networking configuration.

[GoogleSQL query syntax](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/standard-sql/query-syntax)

questions.stem: Which goals align well with Dataplex? (Select TWO)

options.text: Organizing data into lakes and zones
Running governance and data quality tasks

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Dataplex organizes distributed data estates and provides governance and quality capabilities.

**C** is incorrect because it does not replace the warehouse query engine.

**D** is incorrect because container images belong in Artifact Registry.

[Dataplex overview](https://cloud.google.com/dataplex/docs/overview)

questions.stem: Which storage class is typically best for frequently accessed active datasets kept in Cloud Storage?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because the **Standard** storage class is designed for data that is accessed frequently or serves active workloads.

**B** is incorrect because Nearline is intended for data accessed less than once per month.

**C** is incorrect because Coldline targets even less frequent access patterns.

**D** is incorrect because Archive is for the rarest access patterns and the longest retrieval expectations.

[Cloud Storage storage classes](https://cloud.google.com/storage/docs/storage-classes)

questions.stem: Which service is best suited for running managed Spark and Hadoop workloads when a team still wants that ecosystem?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Dataproc is Google Cloud's managed Spark and Hadoop service.

**B** is incorrect because Looker Studio is BI.

**C** is incorrect because Cloud Functions runs event-driven code.

**D** is incorrect because Bigtable is a NoSQL database.

[Dataproc overview](https://cloud.google.com/dataproc/docs/concepts/overview)

questions.stem: Why would a team use a BigQuery view instead of copying data into a second table?

options.text: To reuse query logic without duplicating data

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because views let teams encapsulate SQL logic while continuing to query the underlying data.

**B** is incorrect because durability is unrelated.

**C** is incorrect because views do not create network boundaries.

**D** is incorrect because Pub/Sub ordering is unrelated.

[BigQuery views introduction](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/views-intro)

questions.stem: A data steward wants to inspect text for possible credit card numbers and mask them before release. Which service should they use?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Sensitive Data Protection provides built-in detectors and de-identification methods for sensitive content.

**B** is incorrect because Cloud Build is CI/CD.

**C** is incorrect because Cloud Monitoring tracks metrics.

**D** is incorrect because BI Engine accelerates analytics.

[Sensitive Data Protection overview](https://docs.cloud.google.com/sensitive-data-protection/docs)

questions.stem: Which statements about Looker Studio are true? (Select TWO)

options.text: It can connect to BigQuery data
It is used for dashboards and reports
It is the primary service for training custom models

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Looker Studio is the visualization and reporting layer and can use BigQuery as a source.

**C** is incorrect because model training is not its purpose.

**D** is incorrect because IAM policy management happens elsewhere.

[Looker Studio overview](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/studio/what-you-need-to-use-looker-studio)

questions.stem: Which BigQuery feature allows analysts to query nested repeated arrays in a table?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because `UNNEST` expands arrays so GoogleSQL can work with repeated fields.

**B** is incorrect because `kubectl` is for Kubernetes.

**C** is incorrect because `gsutil rsync` copies objects.

**D** is incorrect because Armor rules are security controls.

[GoogleSQL query syntax](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/standard-sql/query-syntax)

questions.stem: A team must process data in both batch and streaming modes with one programming model. Which choices align with that requirement? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Dataflow is the managed runtime for Apache Beam, which supports both batch and streaming pipelines.

**C** is incorrect because Looker Studio is for dashboards, not pipeline execution.

**D** is incorrect because buckets store data but do not provide a unified programming model.

[Dataflow overview](https://cloud.google.com/dataflow/docs/overview)

questions.stem: A query scans far more bytes than expected because it filters on an unpartitioned timestamp column. Which redesign is likely to help most?

options.text: Partition the table on the relevant timestamp or date column
Move the data to Cloud Storage Archive
Switch the report to Looker Studio
Store the data in Secret Manager

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because partitioning on the filtering column enables partition pruning and usually reduces scanned data.

**B** is incorrect because Archive storage is unrelated.

**C** is incorrect because a BI tool does not change warehouse layout.

**D** is incorrect because Secret Manager is not data storage for analytics.

[Partitioned tables in BigQuery](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/partitioned-tables)

questions.stem: Which are examples of managing data on Google Cloud? (Select TWO)

options.text: Applying data quality and governance policies with Dataplex
Using load balancer health checks as metadata

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because data management includes access control and governance practices such as quality checks and stewardship.

**C** is incorrect because DNS records do not replace analytics languages.

**D** is incorrect because health checks are not metadata governance.

[Dataplex overview](https://cloud.google.com/dataplex/docs/overview)

questions.stem: An analyst wants a no-code dashboard over BigQuery results. Which service is the most direct option?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Looker Studio is designed for report and dashboard authoring over sources including BigQuery.

**B** is incorrect because GKE Autopilot runs containers.

**C** is incorrect because KMS manages encryption keys.

**D** is incorrect because Spanner is a database service.

[Looker Studio overview](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/studio/what-you-need-to-use-looker-studio)

questions.stem: Which services are commonly involved in ingesting data into BigQuery? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because staged files in Cloud Storage and native BigQuery ingestion interfaces are common ingestion building blocks.

**C** is incorrect because private DNS zones are networking resources.

**D** is incorrect because Binary Authorization governs software deployment.

[Load data into BigQuery](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/loading-data)

questions.stem: Which service is designed to trigger jobs based on a cron schedule?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Cloud Scheduler is Google Cloud's managed cron service.

**B** is incorrect because BI Engine accelerates analytics.

**C** is incorrect because Cloud Armor is a security service.

**D** is incorrect because CA Service manages PKI.

[Cloud Scheduler overview](https://cloud.google.com/scheduler/docs/overview)

questions.stem: A team wants to standardize a frequent SQL transformation without materializing another full table. Which object is usually appropriate in BigQuery?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because a view stores query logic and can be reused without duplicating the dataset.

**B** is incorrect because VPC networks manage connectivity.

**C** is incorrect because Cloud NAT handles egress.

**D** is incorrect because Pub/Sub schemas validate messages.

[BigQuery views introduction](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/views-intro)

questions.stem: A steward needs a central place to organize distributed data assets into lakes and zones while applying governance. Which managed capability best matches?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Dataplex is specifically designed to organize and govern distributed data estates using concepts like lakes and zones.

**B** is incorrect because build triggers automate CI/CD.

**C** is incorrect because Firebase Hosting serves web apps.

**D** is incorrect because AlloyDB Omni is unrelated.

[Dataplex overview](https://cloud.google.com/dataplex/docs/overview)

questions.stem: Which tasks can Looker Studio help with? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Looker Studio focuses on visualization, dashboard authoring, and report sharing.

**C** is incorrect because node pools are Kubernetes administration.

**D** is incorrect because VPC Service Controls are security perimeter configuration.

[Looker Studio overview](https://docs.cloud.google.com/looker/docs/studio/what-you-need-to-use-looker-studio)

questions.stem: Which SQL dialect is the default and recommended option for querying BigQuery tables?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because **GoogleSQL** is BigQuery's default and recommended SQL dialect for querying tables.

**B** is incorrect because Legacy SQL still exists for older workloads, but it is not the default or recommended choice for new work.

**C** is incorrect because YAML is a configuration format, not a BigQuery query dialect.

**D** is incorrect because CSS is a styling language, not a SQL dialect.

[GoogleSQL query syntax](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/standard-sql/query-syntax)

questions.stem: Which service would you choose for managed Apache Spark when Dataflow is not the chosen runtime and Spark compatibility is required?
questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because Dataproc is the Google Cloud managed service for Spark and Hadoop workloads.

**B** is incorrect because Looker Studio is BI.

**C** is incorrect because Cloud Armor is WAF/DDoS protection.

**D** is incorrect because IAM Recommender suggests permission optimizations.

[Dataproc overview](https://cloud.google.com/dataproc/docs/concepts/overview)

questions.stem: Why might a team use BigQuery external tables instead of loading every file immediately?

options.text: To query supported external data where it already resides
To guarantee lower cost in every case

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because external tables can be useful when data should stay in external storage but still be queryable from BigQuery.

**B** is incorrect because IAM still applies.

**C** is incorrect because cost depends on the workload.

**D** is incorrect because external tables do not replace the storage service.

[BigQuery external tables](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/external-tables)

questions.stem: Which actions support secure and governed analytics on Google Cloud? (Select TWO)

options.text: Inspect and de-identify sensitive data when needed
Grant dataset editor to every user by default
Publish all raw buckets publicly for convenience

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because secure analytics depends on least-privilege access and appropriate handling of sensitive information.

**C** is incorrect because broad editor access violates least privilege.

**D** is incorrect because public raw buckets create clear data-exposure risk.

[Access control with IAM in BigQuery](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/access-control)

questions.stem: Which BigQuery design choices can help reduce query cost or scanned bytes? (Select TWO)

options.text: Partition tables on common filter columns
Cluster tables on frequently filtered columns
Use Archive class for every Cloud Storage object regardless of use

questions.explanation:
**A** is correct because partitioning and clustering are direct BigQuery table design choices that can reduce scanned data for common access patterns.

**C** is incorrect because Cloud DNS does not store dashboards.

**D** is incorrect because Cloud Storage class selection does not optimize BigQuery native table scans.

[Optimize query computation in BigQuery](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/best-practices-performance-compute)
